title: Xtra Spicy Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A spicy and light Tunisian dish featuring sage and beurre-monte, cooked in a tandoor clay pot, this recipe is a nostalgic and flavorful experience. It is also gluten-free friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the tandoor clay pot over medium heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the sage, garlic, ginger, cumin, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add 1 cup of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours.
Stir in the beurre-monte and continue to simmer for another 30 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional sage if desired.
Traditionally, the tagine is cooked over an open flame, but a stovetop or oven can be used as a substitute.
If using a tandoor clay pot, make sure to heat it slowly and carefully to avoid cracking.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before serving.
